Tests that `Math.floor` returns `NaN` when given `NaN` as input.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that verifies the behavior of the `Math.floor` function when it receives `NaN` (Not-a-Number) as an input. The script uses the `assert` function to check that `Math.floor(NaN)` returns `NaN`, which is the expected behavior according to the ECMAScript specification. This test ensures that the `Math.floor` function correctly handles non-numeric input by returning `NaN`, maintaining consistency with JavaScript's handling of undefined or invalid numerical operations. The script provides narrow functionality, focusing solely on this specific test case.
